title: Build your own level
summary: Levels are plain text and the movement is a handful of tunable numbers, both editable without touching Rust. If you can type, you can make a level.

title: Closing the loop
summary: A finish line, lives that run out, and a state machine tying title to play to win or game over and back. The parts finally become a game you can start, lose, and restart.

title: Blocks, coins, and getting big
summary: Question blocks that answer a headbutt, coins that add up to a life, and a mushroom that turns a fatal hit into a shrink. The pieces that make a level worth exploring.

title: Enemies, stomps, and dying
summary: An enemy that patrols and turns at ledges, a stomp that hinges on who was moving down, and death with a respawn. All tested without ever opening a window.

title: Booting to the title screen
summary: How the first real frame got drawn: verify the ROM, read the title from a headless emulator's video memory, decode the tiles, and render them ourselves. Measured 99.82% match against the emulator.

title: The workspace and how it is driven
summary: What the repo is, the one-task-at-a-time loop that drives it, and the four-module shape of the code.
